behavior_summary: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch is an area/trigger handler that scans the area's item list and performs story-driven scene setup and cleanup when the event fires.

It contains multiple gated flows keyed to global story flags: a 'told dagger' flow (create/destroy dagger-related items and mark toldDaggerDone), a ceremony flow (teleport NPCs, set ceremonySoon, spawn a follow-up routine), a Lithos meeting setup (create and place many items, set lithosMetDone), and tomb/Loth interment handling (destroy/create tomb items).

Selection of targets is done by iterating items, testing item shape/frame and position (Item::getZ/getX/getY), treating NPCs specially (Item::isNpc => Npc::teleport) and otherwise using Item::push/Item::create/Item::pop and engine helpers (FREE::*) to assemble and place scene objects.
The function modifies persistent story state (sets toldDaggerDone, ceremonySoon, lithosMetDone) to prevent repeating sequences and to schedule follow-up behavior.
notable_control_flow: Iterate over the area's item list and for each item: read Z/X/Y, test shape/frame/coords, then branch to destroy/create/teleport logic.
If toldDagger flag is set and toldDaggerDone is not set: remove existing dagger-related items, create/place new dagger items, and set toldDaggerDone.

If haveDagger is set: call resetRef for small refs, set ceremonySoon, teleport NPC(s) to ceremony coords (using FREE::1A85 to obtain/encode coordinates), and spawn U8P_CEMHAT::METHOD::15E7 to continue the ceremony asynchronously.
If lithosMet is set and lithosMetDone is not set: run a bulk item-creation flow using repeated Item::create + FREE::24A7 + Item::pop calls to populate many placed objects, then set lithosMetDone.
If lothInterred is relevant: scan for tomb/Loth items, destroy or replace them as appropriate (inferred tomb cleanup/creation).
open_questions: Exact semantics and data layout of FREE::24A7 and FREE::1A85: they appear to assemble multi-field placement/configuration records but their precise fields and side effects are not fully determined.
Concrete item type IDs, shapes, and frames used in matching/creation steps (the disassembly shows constants but the mapping to in-game item types is inferred rather than canonical).
Precise coordinate mapping and whether Item::pop(uword,uword,ubyte) uses map-tile ids, local XY, or encoded location values returned from FREE::1A85.
Whether METHOD::15E7 is defined in the same script module or another module (it appears to be a follow-up/spawn routine tied to the ceremony but its code is not included here).

Edge conditions around multiple NPCs in the area: whether all matching NPCs are teleported or only the first/closest (looping implies multiple can be handled but exact selection rules need verification).

main_function.name: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch
main_function.purpose: Area/trigger handler that prepares, schedules, or cleans up scene objects and NPC positions to progress multiple story beats (dagger reveal, ceremony, Lithos meeting, Loth interment) when the event fires.
entry_conditions: Triggered by an area/fast-area event (area entry/trigger).
Behavior gated by global story flags such as toldDagger, haveDagger, lithosMet, and lothInterred.

function_map.name: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch
function_map.role: Primary area event handler that scans area items and executes story flows (dagger, ceremony, lithos, tomb).

function_map.name: U8P_CEMHAT::METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Spawned continuation/finalizer for the ceremony sequence (invoked asynchronously after NPC teleportation).

function_map.name: Item::*
function_map.role: Engine item API (destroy/create/push/pop/getX/getY/getZ/isNpc) used to detect, remove, create and place items and to test coordinates and NPC status.

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Engine API used to move NPCs to ceremony/meeting coordinates.

function_map.name: FREE::24A7
function_map.role: Engine helper that assembles/configures multi-field structures for complex item placement; invoked during bulk placement (lithos/tomb flows).

function_map.name: FREE::1A85
function_map.role: Engine helper that computes/returns an encoded coordinate or placement value used for teleport/pop placement.

function_map.name: U8P_CEMHAT::resetRef
function_map.role: Reset/initialize small reference slots prior to scheduling the ceremony.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn an asynchronous continuation to proceed with or finalize the ceremony after NPCs have been teleported and ceremonySoon set.

external_calls.purpose: Remove existing items that conflict with or precede creation of new story objects (cleanup).
Allocate new item instances used to populate the scene (dagger, lithos, tomb pieces).
Push an item state onto an internal stack for later Item::pop placement or transformation.
Finalize placement of a created item at specific coordinates/flags (commonly used after Item::create or FREE::* assembly).
Consume or restore pushed item state; used in some placement/transform sequences.
Query item elevation and coordinates to filter which items to act on during the area scan.
Detect NPC items so that teleportation (Npc::teleport) is used instead of static item placement/destruction.
Move found NPCs to ceremony or meeting coordinates determined by FREE::1A85/constructed values.
Clear or initialize small reference slots before scheduling ceremony-related state.
Assemble a complex multi-field placement/configuration record used repeatedly in the lithos/tomb item creation flow.
Compute/encode a coordinate or placement id used for teleport/pop placement of NPCs or items.

reads.name: toldDagger
reads.likely_effect: Gate whether the 'told dagger' creation/cleanup flow should run; read to decide entry into dagger flow.

reads.name: toldDaggerDone
reads.likely_effect: Checked to avoid repeating the dagger flow if already completed.

reads.name: haveDagger
reads.likely_effect: If set, triggers ceremony scheduling and NPC teleportation flow.

reads.name: lithosMet
reads.likely_effect: Gate whether the Lithos meeting item-creation setup should run.

reads.name: lithosMetDone
reads.likely_effect: Checked to avoid repeating the Lithos meeting setup.

reads.name: lothInterred
reads.likely_effect: Indicates Loth interment state; read to drive tomb-related cleanup/creation flows.

writes.name: toldDaggerDone
writes.likely_effect: Set to mark dagger flow as completed so it does not rerun.

writes.name: ceremonySoon
writes.likely_effect: Set to signal that a ceremony has been scheduled/should occur soon (used to coordinate later behavior).

writes.name: lithosMetDone
writes.likely_effect: Set to mark the Lithos meeting setup as complete and prevent repetition.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch is an area/trigger handler that scans the area's item list and performs story-driven scene setup and cleanup when the event fires.
